completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalPublisher.publish_request is defined as `async def` taking a MessageEnvelope, asserted by TestPublisherSurface.test_publish_request_is_async via asyncio.iscoroutinefunction. Class is exported from forge.adapters.nats.approval_publisher.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _build_approval_details(decision: GateDecision, **context) -> dict produces the eleven-key dict per API §3.2
completion_promises.evidence: _build_approval_details produces exactly the eleven documented keys (build_id, feature_id, stage_label, gate_mode, coach_score, criterion_breakdown, detection_findings, rationale, evidence_priors, artefact_paths, resume_options). Asserted by TestBuildApprovalDetails: returns_exactly_the_eleven_documented_keys, scalar_fields_propagate_from_decision, criterion_breakdown_is_a_plain_dict_copy, detection_findings_serialised_as_list_of_dicts, evidence_priors_use_entity_id_and_summary_subset, kwargs_propagate_verbatim, returned_dict_is_json_serialisable, degraded_decision_emits_none_coach_score (8 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: _derive_risk_level(decision: GateDecision) -> Literal['low', 'medium', 'high'] matches the table in §3.3 exactly
completion_promises.evidence: Helper implements the §3.3 table verbatim: HARD_STOP -> 'high'; MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL -> 'medium'; FLAG_FOR_REVIEW -> 'low' if coach_score >= 0.65 else 'medium'. AUTO_APPROVE raises ValueError (not in table). Asserted by TestDeriveRiskLevel across 7 cases including the 0.65 boundary, sub-threshold, degraded mode (None), HARD_STOP, MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL, and AUTO_APPROVE rejection.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Subject resolves to agents.approval.forge.{build_id} (project-scoped via Topics.for_project if configured)
completion_promises.evidence: _subject_for() calls Topics.resolve(Topics.Agents.APPROVAL_REQUEST, agent_id='forge', task_id=build_id) and wraps via Topics.for_project when self._project is set. Verified by TestPublishRequest.test_subject_resolves_to_agents_approval_forge_build_id (asserts 'agents.approval.forge.<build_id>') and test_project_scope_prefixes_subject (asserts 'finproxy.agents.approval.forge.<build_id>').

completion_promises.criterion_text: Publish failures raise a typed ApprovalPublishError that the caller can catch — failure does NOT swallow silently
completion_promises.evidence: Underlying exceptions are caught and re-raised as ApprovalPublishError (RuntimeError subclass) with .subject and .cause attributes plus __cause__ chain. Asserted by TestPublishRequest.test_publish_failure_wraps_underlying_exception (verifies wrapping, attribute access, and message content).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group E @data-integrity @regression: when the publish call raises, the helper still returns control to the caller without rolling back any state — verified by integration test in TASK-CGCP-011
completion_promises.evidence: Publisher's exception path only logs and raises; it never reaches into caller domain. Asserted at unit level by TestPublishRequest.test_publish_failure_does_not_roll_back_caller_state (caller_state sentinel mapping unchanged after ApprovalPublishError propagates). Full Group E @data-integrity scenario is the integration test in TASK-CGCP-011 as noted in the AC text.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group E @integration: a published request carries enough context for an adapter to render — assertion against the eleven-key dict
completion_promises.evidence: Published envelope round-trips through MessageEnvelope.model_validate and ApprovalRequestPayload.model_validate, recovering payload.details with set(details.keys()) == EXPECTED_KEYS (the eleven §3.2 keys). Asserted by TestPublishedEnvelopeContract.test_published_envelope_round_trips_through_message_envelope and test_envelope_payload_is_serialisable_as_json.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Module is the only place in forge.gating / forge.adapters.nats.approval_* where the details dict shape is constructed
completion_promises.evidence: TestSingleOwnership.test_no_other_module_in_forge_constructs_evidence_priors walks src/forge/**/*.py and asserts the literal 'evidence_priors' appears nowhere except approval_publisher.py. This is a structural CI guard against future scope creep.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors

Files conform to .claude/rules/code-style.md (PEP 8, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, type hints throughout, line length <=88).
